page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48-lssd is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The processor is equipped with a substantial 504 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 with VNNI and BF16 support, alongside Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

The instance is configured with 372 GB of system memory, providing approximately 7.75 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it bundles eight NVMe SSDs totaling 2951 GB of high-speed local capacity. This combination of high-capacity memory and local NVMe storage supports data-intensive operations directly on the physical host, eliminating the latency associated with external network-attached storage volumes.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong performance characteristics. Single-core performance is notable, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores reaching the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth measured at 3863.56 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 178.52 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core processing is solid, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU score of 70422.1 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 19141.0. For database workloads, it handles 88.12 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 22860.29 on Passmark database operations.

The hardware profile is optimized for workloads requiring a balance of high memory capacity, fast local storage, and strong vector math capabilities. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, large-scale Redis deployments, software compilation, and local LLM inference, where its AMX instructions deliver top-tier prompt processing speeds. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bundling of 2951 GB of local NVMe SSDs with 372 GB of RAM on a dedicated 48-vCPU platform provides a dense resource footprint that minimizes the need for separate storage provisioning.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48-lssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 372 GB of RAM and 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across eight drives. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations. In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits top-tier single-core performance, strong memory read bandwidth of up to 3863.56 GB/sec, and high-throughput database capabilities, executing 88.12 million Redis SET operations per second. This hardware configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 372 GB RAM, and 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48-lssd features 48 vCPUs, 372 GB RAM, and 2951 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing, massive RAM, and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Equipped with 372 GB of system memory and 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance and strong memory read bandwidth up to 3863.56 GB/sec
Delivers high-throughput database performance with 88.12 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for in-memory databases, software compilation, and LLM inference
